name: Cccs of Rochester-Chattanooga
description_short: CCCS of Rochester-Chattanooga is a non-profit credit counseling organization offering debt management plans and financial guidance to help individuals regain control of their finances.
description_long: CCCS of Rochester-Chattanooga operates as a non-profit credit counseling service dedicated to assisting individuals struggling with debt management. The organization provides comprehensive debt management plans (DMPs) designed to help consumers consolidate and manage their outstanding debts more effectively. Based on the available documentation, they offer personalized financial guidance and structured repayment programs tailored to individual circumstances. The company emphasizes consumer education and support throughout the debt management process, with dedicated customer service representatives available to address account inquiries and eligibility questions. What distinguishes CCCS of Rochester-Chattanooga is their non-profit status and focus on providing accessible financial counseling rather than profit-driven debt solutions. They appear to handle refund requests transparently, with documented scenarios for overpayment situations, fee adjustments, plan completion refunds, and service changes. The organization maintains clear communication protocols and encourages direct contact for personalized guidance. An honest assessment reveals that detailed information about their specific service offerings, success rates, and fee structures is limited in the available documentation. The website content primarily focuses on refund procedures rather than comprehensive service descriptions, which may reflect their emphasis on customer service accessibility.
pros: Non-profit organization focused on consumer financial welfare rather than profit maximization
Clear refund eligibility criteria including overpayment situations, fee adjustments, and plan completion scenarios
Documented refund timeline varies by payment method (3-15 working days depending on method)
Personalized customer service team available for account-specific guidance and eligibility assessment
Transparent communication about rights and refund procedures with step-by-step request process
Multiple contact methods available (phone and email) for customer inquiries
Accepts various payment methods including credit/debit cards, ACH transfers, money orders, and wire transfers
cons: Limited publicly available information about specific services offered beyond debt management plans
No pricing information or fee structure detailed on provided documentation
Refund processing times are relatively lengthy (3-15 working days depending on payment method)
Website content primarily addresses refund procedures rather than service overview or outcomes
No success rate data or customer outcome metrics provided in available materials
best_for: Individuals struggling with multiple debts seeking structured repayment plans
Consumers who have overpaid or experienced billing issues with their debt management service
People seeking non-profit credit counseling without profit-driven incentives
Those needing personalized financial guidance and account management support
diagnosis: CCCS of Rochester-Chattanooga is best for consumers seeking non-profit debt management counseling and structured repayment plans without profit-driven sales pressure. The primary caveat is that publicly available information focuses heavily on refund procedures rather than service details, so prospective clients should contact the organization directly to understand their specific offerings, fees, and expected outcomes.
services: Debt management plan (DMP) development and administration
Customer service support via phone and email
Communication record management for account disputes
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 5600 Brainerd Rd, Suite C-26, Chattanooga, TN 37411
meta_description: CCCS of Rochester-Chattanooga is a nonprofit credit counseling organization offering debt management plans and financial guidance.
